item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What is we are on "the other side" ?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's exactly what they are doing - Secure, Contain and Protect. Needless scalation is never the way to go.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well regardless of who was right or wrong when someone starts firing nukes at us surely the Foundation can't just do nothing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I upvoted it, even though analyzing the skip on depth eventually makes it become an exercise on suspension of disbelief.

The Foundation routinely exacts protocols to prevent cross-contamination from any and all different dimensions - yet here you see them screwing up what should otherwise be basic rules to handle pandemics, and even before, properly cleaning up their telegraphic cable. IDK, the article overall intent is amazing and it's solid, but something like this makes me wonder if prior to this catastrophe, the Foundation didn't bother hiring a epidemiologist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't understand how they could have such a problem with the flu

That's because both the Foundation and the SCP-1322-A analogue just didn't bother with proper quarantine protocols and actually understading each other's biology - just because the SCP-1322-A "humans" don't show significant deviations from our normal humans, doesn't mean that they are the same. Hence this civilization having made significant advances in physics and engineering but not being unable to do so in biology. The problem here was the Foundation going "Yep, they look like humans, that means we can just send them a flu vaccine without even testing it on one of their baseline humans".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why would the Foundation scalate this when all of this came down to both the Foundation and the SCP-1322-A analogue not enacting proper quarantine protocol? If you pay attention to the logs, the infection came to their universe thanks to the insertion of the telegraphic cable from our end into their universe. Really, the damage has been done, just let them live their last generation without originating more misery.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A very well written piece, although it does raise a question about revenge; whether or not the Foundation from "this" universe would justify retaliatory attacks to protect itself.

If anything, it could make for an interesting concept for a new tale, possibly touching on the ideas of ethics, self-defence etc.
One of the wiki's great masterpieces.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Idk man it did tell us that they arent big on biological sciences but them just using that vaccine on their entire population without testing it and then blaming us just sounds really really stupid

And that part where some dude on the other side has a meltdown like "YOU KILLED US" but then they drop a nuke like up to 100 years later like you seem to be doing fine my guy calm your titties

I dont understand how they could have such a problem with the flu even if they arent big in the medical field they knew how to isolate a sample and properly contain it so its not like they are cavemen
and our vaccine worked so its the same virus but their bodies just had a bad reaction to the vaccine

I just cant imagine a parallel universe foundation being salty over an obvious mistake for centuries and just throwing weak ass attacks through a hole all the time the main dimensions foundation would just try to neutralize them at that point

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This doesn't feel like the Guardian of Forever from Star trek but more like SG1 and the bad revival of SGU, aside from that…GOOD JOB!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"See containment and observation log excerpts below."

It's a two-way war. The documentation in the article doesn't happen to include excerpts describing our side's actions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really want to upvote this scip thanks to it's great writing, fantastic concept and ability to elicit such a large amount of dialogue and theory crafting but one thing stops me. The Foundation has done everything in their power on many occasions to destroy or stymie Keter class scips which are likely to cause K class events. In this scip they do absolutely nothing other than attempt to counteract increasingly deadly attacks (some using technology beyond our ability to replicate and thus reliably counter). The seeming apathy of the Foundation in this regard slightly spoils this scip for me. Probably my most regretful no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Maybe they position their weapons within 5cm of the hole, so it's not hard to hit.

Maybe they take a few months to properly calculate and line up each shot.

Maybe they don't consistently hit it, and we only find out about the 1 shot in 1000 that actually makes it through the portal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The most impressive thing about these attacks is not that they have nanotech, energy beam weapons, or can machinegun slugs with 16 terraloules of kinetic energy.

It's that they can consistently hit a target with a surface area under 1/3 of an inch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: what if the foundation stuck SCP-096 in it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think 682 would make a nice gift for them. Two birds with one stone, right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well they have obviously recovered from the drop in fertility….. but DAMNED if they can't hold a grudge….. looks liked we are there 682 hu?

You know all we would have to do is send them something a little more serious than the flu to wipe them out, they should be nice to us…. thankfully we are a lot nicer than them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Their attempts could easily be countered if things went too far by simply placing SCP-123 around the anomaly. Particle beams, nanomachines, pathogens, and nukes aren't going to get very far when immediately pitted against what might as well be a black hole.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Echoing the sentiments of "yeah, this is fantastic." The interacts between the Foundation and the other world are well thought-out and detailed, and the premise, although arguable generic, presents itself in an outstanding fashion.

This is amazing work. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one. :)

item.title: Still Awesome!
item.content:encoded: Still one of my favorite SCPs to date, like damn did this keep my attention all the way through and I felt such sorrow for the other universe!

item.title: They're not necessarily doomed.
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1322-A organization reports massive, ongoing worldwide upheaval attributable to drop in fertility. Message received indicating fewer than 1,000 live births reported globally in the past 72 days.

1,000 in 72 days isn't much, but it's not nothing. Assuming the effects of the vaccine aren't inherited, which there's no reason they would be, and assuming that birth rate continued, that's roughly 5,000 unaffected babies per year. If the adults have any sense at all, they'll have done their best to ensure that as many of those kids as possible were rounded up, kept away from the virus, and strongly encouraged to start reproducing as soon as they reached sexual maturity. 42 years later, there could well be a sizable and growing population born after the "Last Generation."

Also, upvoted.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: I gotta agree with spikebrennan on this one. The tones of 1322 and Skeeve Online are exceptionally different, and going from 1322 to Skeeve will likely cause cognitive dissonance in the reader. Lovely idea and I appreciate the opportunity for exposure, but it's best not to.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: No.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Given that I wrote both SCP-1322 and "Sic Transit Gloria Mundi" and I'd really like that tale to get more love, I approve.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: I always interpreted that tale as heralding the end of the people on the other side, but now there's no Foundation to contain their revenge.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: All Things Considered shows how things could have gone better had the Foundation never existed.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
I noted that the Tale Skeeve Online depicts the in-game setting to be a world resembling that in SCP-1322 (where in there is sterility plague) and makes mention of 1322 there. I suppose the link can go both ways.

So I propose the addition of an in-line link at here:

SCP-1322-A reports that its analysis indicates that decreases in fertility appear to be a side effect of the Foundation-provided vaccine.

Or a line at the bottom of the collapsible (or after all the text about 1322-A sending stuff to ruin the Foundation's world):

Hostile actions believed to be undertaken by SCP-1322-A is noted to be similar to the backstory of the game called Massy's Big Chance, Multiplayer Edition. However, due to hostility from SCP-1322-A, no investigations can be made.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as described below. Author may revert changes at anytime.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Man, I really love this skip. The Foundation tries to help and borks and entire civilization. What is the saying? The road to Hell is paved with good intentions… Either way the line "YOU KILLED US. YOU DID THIS TO US. IN YOUR CARELESSNESS AND YOUR ARROGANCE YOU HAVE DESTROYED OUR POSTERITY. BUT WE SHALL AVENGE. WE OF THE LAST GENERATION PLEDGE AND VOW THIS. WE WILL FIND A WAY" is really brought into light in the tale Sic Transit Gloria Mundi, in which 1322-A do make good on their vow, in a rather clever way. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

Last communication received from SCP-1322-A. Communication consisted of text reading “YOU KILLED US. YOU DID THIS TO US. IN YOUR CARELESSNESS AND YOUR ARROGANCE YOU HAVE DESTROYED OUR POSTERITY. BUT WE SHALL AVENGE. WE OF THE LAST GENERATION PLEDGE AND VOW THIS. WE WILL FIND A WAY."

To this:

Last communication received from SCP-1322-A. Communication consisted of text reading “YOU KILLED US. YOU DID THIS TO US. IN YOUR CARELESSNESS AND YOUR ARROGANCE YOU HAVE DESTROYED OUR POSTERITY. BUT WE SHALL AVENGE. WE OF THE LAST GENERATION PLEDGE AND VOW THIS. WE WILL FIND A WAY."

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: Re: Where the broken GOD comes from.
item.content:encoded: Yeah, it made plenty of sense to me. And I don't headcanon it, but I do like it.

item.title: Re: Where the broken GOD comes from.
item.content:encoded:
Actually, I think I understand this and it's kind of a cool tinfoil-hat-type theory:

A few of the people on the other side survive by achieving the singularity, crossing the Clark's Law threshold between superscience and magic along the way. They then devote themselves to finding a way to open a wormhole to the Foundation-verse's ancient past, or move the existing one there. Goal: send a great doomsday machine animated by the will of the collective into the Foundationverse's distant past to wipe out life and thus prevent the Foundation from ever existing. Theoretically this would retroactively save their own world at the same time they achieve vengeance.

Their plan failed, however, (and here I fill in the blank the OP left) when they passed through the portal and ran headlong into the Daevite Empire, SCP-1000, the militant Sarkists, and all of the other wonderful nightmares of the Foundationverse's distant past. Carnage ensued and the technological abomination succeeded in accidentally saving the Foundationverse…but the god-machine was broken and scattered in the process.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A really tiny bag of dog poo on fire should be next I think

item.title: Re: Where the broken GOD comes from.
item.content:encoded: I don't get it :/

item.title: Re: Where the broken GOD comes from.
item.content:encoded: …in a way that can be parsed, please?

item.title: Where the broken GOD comes from.
item.content:encoded: Later, they replace their organic parts with mechanical artificiality for longer duration to eliminate us, like nano-mechanical driven by quantum power. Observed by eye, it looks like gears running without power source. Later traveled back to current time line in ancient for wiping us from history, and save their species once for all, but failed & broke into pieces. Loop closed, just: who shall break the god……

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know, while I understand that they're angry at being infertile forever and their race dying off, the correct reaction is not killing the race that accidentally did this to you. If they could do this by accident, imagine what they could on purpose. Even then, situations like this is why you test the medicine first, then administer it.
TL;DR Alternate Universe's reaction is asinine and they were partially at fault for not testing it, even if they were dying from a virus.
Edit: Other than that, nice article

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Funny and sad at the same time. Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reminds of the episode "2010" of Stargaze SG-1 where an alien race gives us advanced medicines that effectively sterilize us,

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They wouldn't have all died, correct?
Statistically, a small percentage would have to be immune to the virus, correct?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: .3.1995 has some present tense issues, but other than that, it's amazing. Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Changed the speed, at long last, to try to improve the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Can't believe I'd never gotten around to reading this before now. Fascinating stuff.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not that they have no medicine or bio, it's that the version of the flu that was introduced never evolved in their world while it's so ubiquitous in ours that most humans have a natural immunity to it. They also appear to be at least somewhat different than normal humans in that a vaccine made them sterile, probably due to the suspension agent or a preservative.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting. However, I looked up the term on Wiktionary on a whim, and it turns out there are a lot more definitions for it than I had known or expected. In particular, "glory hole" is slang for "a military trench", which is essentially what the Site built around the trans-universal wormhole has become: a heavily defended network of fortifications.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Possibly from the Battle Hymn of the Republic?

Mine eyes have seen the glory of the coming of the Lord;
He is trampling out the vintage where the grapes of wrath are stored;
He hath loosed the fateful lightning of His terrible swift sword:
His truth is marching on.

(Chorus)
Glory, glory, hallelujah!
Glory, glory, hallelujah!
Glory, glory, hallelujah!
His truth is marching on.

It's used with a lot of "Now, everyone dies" situations.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They kept sticking things in it and eventually got a disease? It is pretty crude and a poor match for the article, IMO, regardless. Titles don't really matter though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I should've clarified that I know of the vulgar/slang term "glory hole". What I don't get is how the term is relevant to this skip as a title; other skips with such punny titles typically employ an unusual but technically appropiate alternate meaning to one of the words in the pun (either taking a literal meaning rather than the term's intended figurative one, or the inverse).

BTW, wouldn't it be logical if the Object Class read "Keter (formerly Euclid)", to reflect the change in status during the Foundation's containment of the anomaly?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. It's a joke. (NSFW Descriptions)
2. Fear Not is a tale/poem that uses this article. There may be others.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Two things:

  1. Why is this skip titled "Glory Hole"? Where's the "glory" part?
  2. Are there any tales that use this skip, besides the two already mentioned in previous pages of this discussion?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Posted without comment:

http://doors-of-distinction.co.uk/doorknobs-and-handles/handles-in-satin-chrome/scp1322

item.title: Staff Post - Closed
item.content:encoded:
Hi there. You may want to look at the date of the post at which you're replying, as it has been almost three years since the one you're replying to. This is necroposting, and even moreso than that, it is contributing to a discussion that was called to a stop. If you wish to file a complaint about a three-year chain of events, you can either contact the appropriate staff or post in the Complaints Thread. To make it clear, please don't reply to this post if you wish to raise further complaints.

This is a closed staff post. Do not reply to this post for any other reason if you are not staff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love how when the guy actually disagrees with Djoric's passive aggressiveness and wants to know what's up, you pull rank. That's some Orwellian shit right there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also something about a race with advanced weaponry and inter dimensional tech but no medicine or bio is so compelling

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Haahah the data logs so effectively convey those poor bastard's impotent rage I love it
+1

item.title: While the post I am replying to us 1256 days old
item.content:encoded: Their population is 8,791,208,800, or ~8.8 billion. 100/.91 (x*.91=100, find x)= 1098.9010989*8 million.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shark Punching Center

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man. SCP not SPC. Special Pontainment Crocedures…?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has become one of my favorite SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: well, they may be able to live a long time. In physics, it really seems that they are at most 30 years ahead of us, so it's completely plausible that they be 30 years behind us in biological studies, so somewhat moden medicine could be plausible

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Has no one noticed that because of the temporal shift affecting SPC-2002, that it might be from this parallel universe? They did mention a disease that cause sterility in the SPC-2002's ship log and recorded message for Foundation personnel. From what I can tell, the log says they (the ship's inhabitants) found a way to cure the sterility and create people from fetuses in with older egg and semen samples, and then put them into a dormant state to help build an immunity to another disease… could that possibly be the regular influenza strain mentioned in this SPC log? Who knows?
